articles.title: Takeaways from the Democratic National Committee’s long-awaited autopsy report on 2024 election
articles.description:
There's been no shortage of criticism toward Harris' campaign after her defeat. Some Democrats think she spent too much time campaigning with Republicans like Liz Cheney, others think she lacked a strong economic message.

The post Takeaways from the Democratic National Committee’s long-awaited autopsy report on 2024 election appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Democratic National Committee's report criticizes Kamala Harris' campaign strategies after 2024 election defeat.
Some Democrats believe Harris spent too much time with Republicans like Liz Cheney during her campaign.
The report highlights the need for a stronger economic message to engage Boston voters effectively.

articles.title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
articles.description:
We priced out three hypothetical Cape Cod getaways for Memorial Day weekend: a day trip, a couple's escape, and a family vacation for four.

The post Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Explore three getaway options for Boston residents heading to Cape Cod this Memorial Day weekend.
Day trip costs approximately $100, while a couple's escape averages $300 for the weekend.
Family vacation for four could reach around $800, impacting local travel budgets.

articles.title: ‘I failed’: Woman at center of sprawling Minnesota fraud gets nearly 42-year prison sentence
articles.description:
“We will claw back every dollar you have stolen from the American people,” Assistant Attorney General Colin McDonald said, noting that the government sent more prosecutors and agents to Minnesota this year.

The post ‘I failed’: Woman at center of sprawling Minnesota fraud gets nearly 42-year prison sentence appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Minnesota woman sentenced to 42 years for large-scale fraud scheme.
Assistant Attorney General Colin McDonald emphasized efforts to recover stolen funds.
Increased federal resources may affect fraud cases in Boston and surrounding areas.
